checks.a_analysis: The output JSON accurately reflects the source document's text. No fabricated headings, body text, or concluding remarks were found. All text in the 'sections' array corresponds to the original PDF content.
The document contains no phone numbers, emails, or URLs. Names of individuals listed in the attendee sections match the source exactly with no fabrication of contact details.
The conversion includes the full single page of the source. All primary sections (Headers, Attendees, Absent, Staff, and Agenda Items I-IV) are present in the output.
The attendee lists were converted into JSON list items. The count for each list matches the source document exactly: 12 members present, 2 members absent, and 8 staff present. No data rows were lost.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The main body text of the minutes is fully preserved. While the red 'APPROVED' stamp and the small header/footer lines (containing the approval date and page number) are not present in the semantic sections, these are typical exclusions for structural JSON conversion. The core informational content of the meeting remains intact.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output matches the source wording precisely. No meaning-altering changes were found. The JSON correctly identified 'Chairman' where the provided raw OCR text had a typo ('Chariman'). All dates and times within the meeting record are correct.

checks.a_analysis: The content in the JSON output matches the source document's sections without duplication. Each list and paragraph appears exactly as many times as it does in the source.

The heading hierarchy is logical and sequential (H1 to H2 to H3). 'MINUTES' is correctly identified as H1, the board meeting title as H2, and the attendance section headers as H3. No levels are skipped.

There are no formal tables in the source document. The attendance section, which is laid out in columns, is correctly represented as structured lists in the output, which is an appropriate conversion for accessibility.

The output follows a logical reading order. It groups the attendance sections (Present, Absent, Staff, Others) before proceeding to the numbered agenda items. Although 'Staff Present' is visually to the right of 'Members Present', reading the document column-by-column for these lists is a standard and acceptable accessibility practice.

The agenda items I, II, III, and IV are presented as a numbered list in the source document. In the JSON output, these have been flattened into individual paragraph elements (reading_order 14-18) rather than being encapsulated in a list structure. Additionally, item III consists of two paragraphs in the source; the second paragraph ('Mr. Greene made the motion...') is rendered as a standalone sibling paragraph in the output (reading_order 17), losing its structural connection/nesting within agenda item III.
checks.b_evidence_source:
I. Call to Order...
II. Roll Call...
III. Senate Bill 600 - [Paragraph 1]
[Paragraph 2 starting with 'Mr. Greene made the motion...']
IV. Adjourn...
checks.b_evidence_output: Elements 14, 15, 16, 17, and 18 are all 'type': 'paragraph' at the same structural level.

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains no hyperlinks, and the output correctly reflects this by not including any link elements.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization and flow of the original document. While the source PDF uses a two-column layout for the attendee lists ('Members Present' and 'Staff Present'), the HTML converts these into sequential single-column lists. This is a standard responsive HTML layout choice and does not negatively impact the user's ability to find information. The header information, numbered sections, and footer content are all present in the expected logical order. The absence of the red 'APPROVED' watermark is expected as it is a decorative background element.
The source document does not contain any formal tables. The lists of attendees are presented in columns but are not structured as tables. The HTML correctly renders these as clean lists.

The meaningful logos from the source PDF (the large 'NC DIT 911 Board' logo at the top and the smaller version at the bottom) are both present in the HTML output. In the HTML, they have been grouped together at the bottom of the document, which is an acceptable practice for decorative branding elements to keep them out of the primary content reading flow. No images appear broken.

There is no duplicated body content. The appearance of headings in the 'Contents' sidebar and then again in the main body is an intentional accessible design feature (navigation landmarks) and not a defect. The attendee lists are continuous and do not repeat content. The two logos at the bottom correspond to the two logos present in the source PDF.
